question_text: Two electromagnets are made using identical copper wire coils wrapped around steel cores. Electromagnet X has 200 coils and is connected to a 6V battery. Electromagnet Y has 400 coils and is connected to the same 6V battery. All other factors are identical. Which electromagnet will have the stronger magnetic field and why?
correct_answer: Electromagnet Y will be stronger because it has twice as many coils, so the magnetic field from each turn adds together to create a stronger overall field.
explanation: The strength of an electromagnet depends on the number of coils (turns) in the wire. More turns mean more magnetic field lines are produced, so electromagnet Y with 400 turns will be significantly stronger than electromagnet X with 200 turns, even though they use the same voltage.
